title: Design System: Kha Dinh — Academic Portfolio

display.purpose: Page-level name and major section openers at lightest weight.

title.purpose: Publication titles, project names — the workhorse heading level.

body.purpose: All prose: bio, abstract excerpts, post content. Max 70ch.

label.purpose: Profile email detail only. One instance per page — a signature, not a system.

motion.name: ease-state
motion.purpose: Default easing for interactive state changes (link hover, nav active). 150ms duration.

components.name: Publication Entry
components.description: Primary content unit. Structured text, not a card. Title + authors + venue in a column.
components.html:
Hardware-Assisted Compartmentalization Models
Kha Dinh Duy, Hojoon Lee
ACM CCS 2023 — Distinguished Paper Award
components.css: .ds-pub-entry { padding: 12px 0; border-top: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.1); font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; } .ds-pub-entry:hover { background: transparent; } .ds-pub-title { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 300; font-size: 1.125rem; line-height: 1.4; color: #000000; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-pub-title a { color: #0f62fe; text-decoration: none; } .ds-pub-title a:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-pub-authors { font-weight: 300; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #000000; margin-bottom: 2px; } .ds-pub-venue { font-weight: 300; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #828282; }

components.name: Navigation Link
components.description: Top nav item. IBM Plex Sans weight 300. Color shift on active/hover, no background fill.
components.html: aboutpublicationscv
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 24px; align-items: center; } .ds-nav-link { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 300; font-size: 1rem; color: #000000; text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #0f62fe; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-active { color: #0f62fe; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f62fe; outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Inline Link
components.description: Body text link in Newsreader. Reference Blue, underline on hover only.
components.html: Research on software compartmentalization in constrained environments.
components.css: .ds-body { font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 300; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.7; color: #000000; } .ds-link { color: #0f62fe; text-decoration: none; transition: text-decoration 100ms; } .ds-link:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f62fe; outline-offset: 1px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: IBM Plex Sans weight 300. No color accent. Hierarchy through size only.
components.css: .ds-headline { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 300; font-size: 1.5rem; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: -0.005em; color: #000000; margin-bottom: 16px; border: none; padding: 0; }

components.name: Profile Label
components.description: Audiowide for the profile email detail. One instance per page — signature, not system.
components.css: .ds-profile-label { font-family: 'Audiowide', monospace; font-weight: 400; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #828282; }

narrative.overview:
This system is built like a well-authored academic paper: the typography does the heavy lifting, the structure is immediately legible, and nothing on the page competes with the argument. IBM Plex Sans carries the headings with sparse, technical authority — weight 200-300, optical-size kerning, no decorative flourish. Newsreader handles the body: an optical-size serif that reads like printed editorial at every size.

Color is disciplined to a single accent: Reference Blue (#0f62fe). It appears on links, citations, and interactive states, never as decoration. Its rarity is the point.

The system explicitly rejects: generic al-folio defaults, flashy portfolio theatrics, and the corporate/LinkedIn register. The goal is a site that feels built by the person who designed the compartmentalization model, not by someone who downloaded a theme.
keyCharacteristics: Typography-led: IBM Plex Sans / Newsreader pairing is the signature, not an accent color
Flat surfaces: no shadow vocabulary, no depth theater
Accent restraint: Reference Blue at ≤10% surface coverage
800px max-width: column width chosen for reading, not filling a screen
Dual-theme first: both light and dark modes designed intentionally

rules.name: The Reference Rule
rules.body: Reference Blue appears on interactive elements only. No colored section headings, no accent borders, no decorative usage. When the eye lands on blue, it means 'this is a link or citation.' Diluting this signal anywhere dilutes it everywhere.

rules.name: The Weight Floor Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Sans appears at weight 200 or 300 only. Weight 400+ collapses the contrast between headings and body text.

rules.name: The Optical Size Rule
rules.body: Newsreader is an optical-size font. Always load with font-optical-sizing: auto. Without this, body text looks slightly too heavy and captions look wrong.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. No hover shadows, no float effects. If an element needs to signal interactivity, use color or underline, not elevation.

rules.name: The Anti-Card Rule
rules.body: Publication lists, project lists, and news items are not cards. They are structured text. No border-radius + box-shadow + background-color on list entries.

dos: Do use IBM Plex Sans at weight 200-300 for headings only.
Do limit Reference Blue (#0f62fe) to interactive states: links, citations, nav active, focus rings.
Do use thin 1px dividers to separate list entries — structure through line, not card borders.
Do set font-optical-sizing: auto on Newsreader at every size.
Do keep max-width at 800px for 65-75ch body line length.
Do treat Audiowide as a signature detail: one instance per page (profile email).
Do support dark mode as a first-class design variant.
donts: Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on any element.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient).
Don't use Reference Blue as a section heading color, background fill, or decorative element.
Don't use cards for publication lists, project lists, or news items.
Don't add scroll-driven animations, hero entrance effects, or floating UI elements.
Don't use generic al-folio defaults without intentional override.
Don't use Audiowide for anything other than the profile email detail.
Don't use IBM Plex Sans at weight 400 or above in headings.
Don't design for the LinkedIn register: no achievement-bullet formatting, no impact-metrics typography.
